source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineering Internship - Fall 2026
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe
At Vital Lyfe, we are dedicated to solving global water challenges through innovative desalination and water treatment solutions. This is an opportunity to join a passionate, multidisciplinary team tackling one of the world’s most critical challenges.

Mission
We’re looking for passionate and driven interns to join our mission and gain hands-on experience working on impactful projects.



What You'll Do

 - Collaborate on the design and optimization of high-pressure systems.

 - Assist in prototyping, testing, and refining lightweight, energy-efficient mechanical components.

 - Analyze materials and system designs to ensure reliability and performance.
   
   
   
   Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Lifting up to 50 lbs



What We’re Looking For

Education: Currently pursuing a degree in Mechanical Engineering.

Skills: Experience or coursework in relevant fields (e.g., fluid dynamics, circuit design, thermodynamics, material science).

Attributes:

 - A passion for solving real-world problems.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 - Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
   
   

Why Join Us?

 - Impact: Work on technology that addresses one of humanity’s most pressing challenges.

 - Hands-On Experience: Tackle real-world projects and gain invaluable industry knowledge.

 - Mentorship: Learn from a team of seasoned engineers and innovators.

 - Growth Opportunities: Contribute to a growing startup and help shape its future.

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

Vital Lyfe is a tech company redefining water autonomy through innovation — creating a new category of personal water-making technology built to scale where infrastructure can’t.

Mission

Design the mechanical components of the system, focusing on pumps, valves, and pressure vessel. Build components that are reliable, efficient, and designed for diverse environments and use cases.



What You'll Do

 - Own consumer hardware from conceptual design through high-volume production and public launch.

 - Develop design criteria, collaborate on requirements, and drive system-level optimization.

 - Test and iterate on products to ensure high reliability and performance in all environments and use cases.

 - Design and develop production lines for manufacturing your product, including robot end effector design, process development, control system design, automation programming, and more.

 - Refine production systems to achieve high-rate, high-yield, and reliable manufacturing processes.

 - Establish an efficient product lifecycle, from supply chain to final integration, balancing cost, complexity, and quality.

 - Collaborate with partner teams to design and implement efficient in-house production lines.

What We're Looking For

 - Bachelor’s degree in an engineering discipline.

 - Proficiency in CAD and FEA software packages.

 - 1+ years of professional experience in mechanical/electromechanical design, analysis, testing, and/or manufacturing.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in high-volume manufacturing or consumer hardware production.

 - Knowledge of pumps, valves or other mechanical components

 - Knowledge of automation programming and process development.

 - Strong problem-solving skills with a focus on innovation and efficiency.

 - Ability to collaborate across multidisciplinary teams and drive projects to completion.

Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Lifting up to 50 lbs
   
   

Why Join Vital Lyfe?

 - Work on high-impact problems with global relevance

 - Help scale breakthrough technology in water security and climate resilience

 - Early ownership in a high-growth, engineering-led startup

 - Join a tight-knit, mission-driven team in a fast-paced environment

 - Build long-term career equity as we grow

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

Vital Lyfe is a tech company redefining water autonomy through innovation — creating a new category of personal water-making technology built to scale where infrastructure can’t.

What You'll Do

 - Design, develop and optimize manufacturing processes and production lines for our desalination systems—from first builds through high-rate production.

 - Lead DFM/DFA initiatives: tooling, fixtures, robot end-effectors, conveyors, automation programming, control systems.

 - Work cross-functionally to select equipment, define cycle-times, yield targets, cost-per-unit, quality metrics.

 - Develop work instructions, process flows, layout designs; implement and validate manufacturing methods for new systems.

 - Own continuous-improvement efforts: reduce cost, increase throughput, improve reliability and uptime in a fast-moving hardware startup environment.

 - Collaborate with procurement, quality, test, integration teams and external vendors/suppliers to ensure smooth scale-up & ramp-out of manufacturing.

What We’re Looking For
Required:

 - Bachelor’s in Engineering (Mechanical, Manufacturing, Industrial or equivalent).

 - 2+ years professional experience in manufacturing engineering, process development or production optimization (including internships).

 - Strong CAD (SolidWorks / other) experience and familiarity with manufacturing automation, tooling & fixture design.

 - Hands-on mindset: comfortable designing the process and also working with operators & equipment to validate and refine.

 - Strong problem-solving skills, ability to operate in ambiguous/higher-growth environment, clear communicator across teams.

Preferred:

 - Experience in consumer hardware production, or systems with high-volume manufacturing.

 - Knowledge of pumps, valves, pressure vessels, fluid systems or broadly mechanical-hardware manufacturing.

 - Experience with automation programming, conveyors, robotics, fluid/pressure equipment.

 - Familiarity with Lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, statistical process control (SPC).

 - Experience in a hardware startup or mission-driven organization.

Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Lifting up to 50 lbs.
   
   

Why Join Vital Lyfe?

 - Work on high-impact problems with global relevance

 - Help scale breakthrough technology in water security and climate resilience

 - Early ownership in a high-growth, engineering-led startup

 - Join a tight-knit, mission-driven team in a fast-paced environment

 - Build long-term career equity as we grow

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

Vital Lyfe is a tech company redefining water autonomy through innovation, creating a new category of personal water making technology built to scale where infrastructure can’t.

The Mission

Own and build the core mechanical systems behind our product; pumps, valves, pressure vessels, and fluid-handling assemblies & take them from early concepts to production at scale. This is a hands on, high-ownership role for someone who wants to design, test, break, fix, and ship hardware.

What You’ll Do

 - Own major mechanical subsystems from concept through production ramp and launch.

 - Design, prototype, and validate pumps, valves, pressure vessels, and related mechanical components.

 - Drive system-level tradeoffs across performance, reliability, manufacturability, and cost.

 - Build and run test plans to validate performance across real-world environments and edge cases.

 - Work closely with electrical, firmware, industrial design, and manufacturing to turn ideas into shipped product.

 - Help design and improve manufacturing processes and production equipment, including fixtures, tooling, and automation concepts.

 - Iterate fast; identify problems, propose solutions, and implement changes without heavy process overhead.

 - Support supplier selection, part qualification, and production ramp.

 - Mentor junior engineers as the team grows (without becoming detached from hands-on work).

What We’re Looking For

 - Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience designing and shipping mechanical or electromechanical hardware.

 - Strong CAD and FEA skills applied to real, production-intent designs.

 - Experience taking products from prototype to manufacturing.

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and making tradeoffs with incomplete information.

 - Bias toward action, ownership, and practical engineering solutions.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with consumer hardware or high-volume manufacturing.

 - Background in pumps, valves, pressure systems, or fluid mechanics.

 - Exposure to automation, manufacturing process development, or production equipment.

 - Startup or early-stage company experience.

Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Lifting up to 50 lbs.

Why Join Vital Lyfe?

 - Work on high-impact problems with global relevance

 - Help scale breakthrough technology in water security and climate resilience

 - Early ownership in a high growth, engineering led startup

 - Join a tight knit, mission driven team in a fast-paced environment

 - Build long term career equity as we grow

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Water Lab Technician
source_jobs.description:
Mission
The Water Laboratory & Membrane Testing Technician will be a hands-on partner to our engineering and R&D teams, transforming ideas and designs into validated, real-world performance. You will play a central role in characterizing water quality and testing membrane performance to ensure our portable water treatment devices are safe, reliable, and high performing. This includes running and improving test methods, operating and maintaining analytical instruments, and executing membrane test protocols that directly influence product design decisions and quality standards.
The ideal candidate is practical, precise, and curious, with a strong aptitude for laboratory work, basic mechanical systems, and data-driven problem solving. You are hands-on, flexible, quick to learn new methods and instruments, comfortable in a fast-paced development environment, and bring a proactive, can-do attitude to supporting our mission of delivering clean water through innovative portable solutions.

What You Will Do

 - Perform routine water quality tests (pH, EC/TDS, turbidity, hardness, alkalinity, major ions, etc.).

 - Operate water characterization instruments (e.g., ICP, UV-Vis, turbidity meter, titrator).

 - Prepare samples, reagents, standards, and calibration solutions.

 - Operate membrane test rigs for RO/NF/UF/MF to measure flux, rejection, and performance.

 - Set up and monitor membrane tests, including feed preparation and operating conditions.

 - Conduct fouling and cleaning studies and support root-cause analysis.

 - Record test conditions and results accurately in lab records and digital systems.

 - Prepare concise test summaries and basic technical reports.

 - Maintain and perform basic troubleshooting on lab and test equipment.

 - Follow lab safety procedures, including PPE use, chemical handling, and waste disposal.

 - Help keep the lab organized, clean, and well stocked with chemicals and consumables.


What We Are Looking For

 - Someone hands-on, practical, and detail-oriented, who enjoys working in the lab with instruments, water samples, and membrane systems.

 - Someone who is eager to learn, can follow procedures, and can work closely with engineers and the team to improve tests and support product development.


Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in a water analysis or environmental testing laboratory.

 - Experience operating ICP and other water characterization instruments.

 - Experience with membrane filtration systems (RO/NF/UF/MF).

 - Knowledge of standard water testing methods (e.g., ISO/ASTM or similar).

 - Basic understanding of water chemistry and membrane performance concepts.

 - Ability to work with pumps, valves, and pressurized systems (or willingness to learn).

 - Proficiency with basic data analysis and report preparation.

· Proficient with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.

 - High attention to detail, good communication, and ability to work in a team.


Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Must be able to lift 50 lbs. unassisted.

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Must be able to climb ladders and be comfortable working in tight spaces.

 - Must be able to move repetitively, bend or twist, and use hands to handle, control, or feel objects, tools or controls.

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Mission

We are looking for a high ownership Recruiter to help build the team that will define the future of water autonomy. This role is not just about filling roles, it's about building the company. You will work directly with founders, engineers, and leaders to identify, attract, and close the kind of talent that changes industries. You will play a critical role in shaping the team that builds Vital Lyfe.

What You'll Do

 - Manage full-cycle recruiting across a variety of roles

 - Partner with hiring managers to understand business needs and define candidate profiles

 - Partner with interviewers to design effective interview processes and evaluation frameworks

 - Source, screen, and engage candidates through multiple channels

 - Coordinate interviews and keep the process moving smoothly and efficiently

 - Maintain accurate candidate data and recruiting workflows in the applicant tracking system (ATS)

 - Ensure a thoughtful candidate experience from first contact through offer

 - Support offer development, reference checks, and pre-employment steps as needed

 - Help improve recruiting processes, workflows, and overall talent operations as we scale

 - Act as a strong ambassador for the company and our culture throughout the hiring process

 - Continuously build strong talent pipelines ahead of hiring demand

What We're Looking For

We are looking for someone who thrives in early-stage environments and is excited to help build a company from the ground up. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to have a meaningful impact in a growing company, help shape the recruiting experience, and play a key role in building strong teams.

Required

 - 1-2 years of recruiting experience, including full-cycle recruiting

 - Experience working in an applicant tracking system (ATS)

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Excellent communication and relationship building skills

 - A genuine people person who builds trust quickly

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt in a growing, changing environment

 - Self starter mindset with strong follow through and good judgment

 - Positive, solutions oriented approach and willingness to roll up your sleeves

 - Ability to operate independently in fast-moving environments

Preferred

 - Experience recruiting for engineering, hardware, or technical roles

 - Experience at high-growth startups or rapidly scaling companies

 - Comfort working directly with founders and senior leadership

 - Track record of identifying and closing high-impact talent

 - Creative sourcing techniques and strong candidate engagement skills

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineering Internship - Fall 2026
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

Vital Lyfe is a tech company redefining water autonomy through innovation, creating a new category of personal water making technology built to scale where infrastructure can’t.



The Mission
We're looking for a Software Engineering Intern to help build the software behind a high-demand consumer hardware product. We're spinning up manufacturing from scratch, scaling fast, and the software you work on will directly affect how we build, track, and deliver thousands of units.

You'll work alongside senior engineers on the tools, data systems, and infrastructure that power our operations and help us scale.

What You'll Do
• Build and improve internal tools that give our engineering and operations teams real-time visibility into production and manufacturing workflows
• Write integrations and scripts that connect systems and keep data reliable across the company
• Design and maintain dashboards that help teams understand operational data and make better decisions
• Assist with backend and cloud infrastructure work as we continue scaling our services
• Debug issues, write tests, and ship improvements alongside senior engineers

What We're Looking For
• Strong programming fundamentals and comfort learning new systems quickly
• Experience with at least one modern programming language such as Python or TypeScript
• Interest in software that supports real-world products and operations
• Familiarity with APIs, web applications, or cloud-based systems
• Practical and curious, with a habit of taking ownership and following through
• Able to break down a problem, work through it, and know when to ask for help
• Excited to work in a fast-paced environment where software and hardware intersect

Nice to Have
• Experience supporting or contributing to live backend systems
• Background in building user-facing interfaces with React or a similar frontend framework
• Exposure to SQL, structured data formats, and dashboards or data visualization tools
• Familiarity with AWS or cloud infrastructure
• Interest in manufacturing, hardware-software integration, or IoT systems

Why Join Vital Lyfe?
• Work on high-impact problems with global relevance
• Contribute to technology addressing water security and climate resilience
• Tackle meaningful problems at the intersection of software, hardware, and operations
• See your work directly affect how we build, ship, and improve real-world products
• Learn quickly in a collaborative environment with broad exposure across engineering

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Automation and Controls Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

Vital Lyfe is a tech company redefining water autonomy through innovation, creating a new category of personal water making technology built to scale where infrastructure can’t.

What You'll Do

 - Design, develop, commission, and manage automation and controls projects for manufacturing, product testing, reliability testing, and end-of-line validation.

 - Help define Vital Lyfe’s controls strategy, including what PLC, HMI, SCADA, data acquisition, fieldbus, and factory software systems we should standardize on as we scale.

 - Architect, write, and debug PLC and controls code that is organized, documented, maintainable, reusable, and appropriate for high-volume manufacturing.

 - Design and commission automated equipment and test systems involving pumps, motors, valves, sensors, pressure, flow, thermal systems, and electrical instrumentation.

 - Keep our factory safe and reliable by implementing compliant safety systems for automated equipment, motion systems, pressure systems, and electro-mechanical assemblies.

 - Generate simple HMI screens, electrical designs, documentation, schedules, and budgets that help teams operate, troubleshoot, and scale factory systems.

What We're Looking For

 - Bachelor’s degree in engineering, physics, computer science, or a related technical discipline.

 - 5+ years of experience in industrial automation, factory automation, automated test systems, or controls system design and implementation.

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and making tradeoffs with incomplete information.

 - Bias toward action, ownership, and practical engineering solutions.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Ability to help select the right automation architecture, controls platform, and factory software tools for a fast-moving manufacturing environment.

 - Experience with electromechanical systems, automated test stands, manufacturing equipment, reliability rigs, or end-of-line test systems.

 - Electrical sizing of motors, cables, circuit protection, VFDs, motor starters, drives, and other motion systems.

 - Design of fail-safe electrical circuits using safety PLCs, safety-rated relays, interlocks, e-stops, and other machine safety devices.

 - Hands-on troubleshooting experience using tools such as multimeters, oscilloscopes, EMI probes, and industrial network diagnostic tools.

 - Proficiency understanding and creating electrical schematics, panel layouts, wiring diagrams, I/O lists, and single-line diagrams.

 - Experience with fieldbus networks, serial communication, Ethernet communication, and industrial protocols such as EtherCAT, Profinet, Ethernet/IP, Modbus, or CAN.

 - Hands-on experience designing and commissioning high-quality electrical panels and electro-mechanical systems.

 - Knowledge of NEC, NFPA, OSHA, UL508A, and related control system safety standards.

 - PLC programming experience; Beckhoff TwinCAT, Rockwell, Siemens, Codesys, Omron, or similar experience is valuable.

 - Experience with HMI, SCADA, MQTT, OPC-UA, Ignition, SQL databases, machine health monitoring, and factory data systems.

 - Experience applying industrial software to mechanical assemblies, electrical systems, sensors, pneumatics, hydraulics, pumps, valves, and instrumentation.

 - Ability to safely check out interdisciplinary systems prior to production implementation.

 - Startup, pilot-line, or high-rate manufacturing experience preferred.

Physical Job Requirements - Essential Functions

 - Hands on work on development hardware.

 - Long periods of standing, crouching, walking.

 - Lifting up to 50 lbs.

Why Join Vital Lyfe?

 - Work on high-impact problems with global relevance

 - Help scale breakthrough technology in water security and climate resilience

 - Early ownership in a high growth, engineering led startup

 - Join a tight knit, mission driven team in a fast-paced environment

 - Build long term career equity as we grow

source_jobs.title: Strategic Growth Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Vital Lyfe

At Vital Lyfe, we are on a mission to solve global water scarcity by creating innovative, desalination systems. This is an opportunity to join a passionate, multidisciplinary team tackling one of the world’s most critical challenges.

Mission

Drive international market activation, leading the business's expansion into new global markets and owning revenue growth across these channels. Direct next generation project management, building the go-to-market strategy and execution roadmap for next generation products across the industrial and commercial sectors. Establish and grow strategic partnerships, including government and commercial alliances, to accelerate market entry and unlock new revenue opportunities.

What You'll Do

· Manage international market activations and navigate regulatory requirements when applicable for all Vital Lyfe product portfolio

· Conduct in-depth market research to assess demand, the competitive landscape, and any barriers to entry into new markets.

· Work cross functionally with Vital Lyfe’s internal teams to align market activation and development efforts with technical capabilities, product roadmaps, and compliance requirements

· Effectively develop and grow a network of supportive stakeholders across the public and private sectors that can aid in promoting Vital Lyfe’s growth in region

· Own the Go-To-Market strategy of Vital Lyfe’s next generation product line, though early prospective customer engagements and funnel growth

· Build and grow the pipeline of commercial opportunities to convert into revenue generating opportunities ahead of 2027 product launch

· Project manage the pilots of first prospective customers of Vital Lyfe’s industrial and commercial solutions

· Take a strategic role in the business, informing pricing strategy, product development and roadmap, and business growth decisions

· Build strategic partnerships with key partners and government entities, aligned with Vital Lyfe’s growth targets.

· Build strong relationships to understand customer requirements, manage expectations, and identify opportunities for growth.

What We're Looking For

· Bachelor’s degree in engineering or business; OR high school diploma/equivalency certificate with 5+ years of professional experience.

· 6+ years professional experience in market access, consulting, project management, business development, or similar role.

· Success launching products across multiple international markets

· Knowledge or experience in navigating international regulatory environments is a plus.

· International customer experience along with foreign language abilities is a plus.

· Proven ability to manage multiple projects and deliver under tight timelines and resource constraints.

· Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to go beyond the apparent answers to uncover root causes and solutions.

· Industry experience in water treatment technology, consumer products, or water utility is a plus.

· Demonstrated ability to capture and incorporate feedback from customers into actionable improvements.

· Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience crafting and presenting professional presentations.

· Empathy, active listening, and resilience in managing relationships.

Why Join Vital Lyfe?

· Work on high-impact problems with global relevance

· Help scale breakthrough technology in water security and climate resilience

· Early ownership in a high-growth, engineering-led startup

· Join a tight-knit, mission-driven team in a fast-paced environment

· Build long-term career equity as we grow

Location Requirement: Ability to work 100% onsite in Torrance, CA (remote or hybrid work will not be considered)



Vital Lyfe is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Vital Lyfe is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran st

notes: Highly technical roles, rapid hiring pace, and innovative product focus suggest a startup with strong engineering roots.
